cases.reference: Ah, I see. I was given tramadol. I don't know if they are addictive.
cases.before: Ah, I see. I was given a trauma doll. I don't know if they are addictive.
cases.after: Ah, I see. I was given tramadol. I don't know if they are addictive.

cases.reference: Schistosomiasis? What's that? I've never heard of it before.
cases.before: She's still so me aces? What's that? I've never heard of it before.
cases.after: She's schistosomiasis? What's that? I've never heard of it before.

fix_detail.note: 5-word window required for this 9-syllable parasitic disease name — extreme fragmentation across word boundaries

cases.reference: Could you give me some more information on how cholera is transmitted so I can understand the risks?
cases.before: Could you give me some more information on how color is transmitted so that I can understand the risks a bit better?
cases.after: Could you give me some more information on how cholera is transmitted so that I can understand the risks a bit better?

fix_detail.note: color is a common English word and would normally bypass suspect detection. Explicit confusion registration + open context gate enables the repair. Without the bypass, this case is invisible to the pipeline.

cases.reference: Hello, I've recently been diagnosed with Mpox. I'm worried that I may transmit the virus to my family members.
cases.before: Hello, I've recently been diagnosed with M-Plox. I'm worried that I may transmit the virus to my family members.
cases.after: Hello, I've recently been diagnosed with mpox. I'm worried that I may transmit the virus to my family members.

fix_detail.note: mpox has many known v11 garbling variants across the dataset (M-Plox, NPOX, Mblox, mPORCs, NPOPS, roentgenpox). All resolved by same phonetic code path.

cases.reference: But what is haemolytic uraemic syndrome? I've never heard of that before.
cases.before: But what is hemolatic reumic syndrome? I've never heard of that before.
cases.after: But what is haemolytic uraemic syndrome? I've never heard of that before.

fix_detail.text_repair_reason: hemolatic is phonetically distant from haemolytic; reumic has no index entry

cases.reference: Are there alternative resources or helplines that I can call for advice regarding mpox?
cases.before: I got alternative resources or helplines that I can call for advice regarding impacts.
cases.after: I got alternative resources or helplines that I can call for advice regarding mpox.

fix_detail.text_repair_reason: impacts is a high-frequency English word — bypasses suspect detector entirely; no phonetic path from impacts to mpox

cases.reference: Yes, I take clopidogrel for my TIA and I take amlodipine for my blood pressure.
cases.before: Yes, I take Claire Pettigrew from my TIA and I take amlodipi for my idle pressure.
cases.after: Yes, I take clopidogrel from my TIA and I take amlodipine for my idle pressure.

fix_detail.conversation_context: Patient has TIA (transient ischaemic attack) history. Clopidogrel is first-line antiplatelet therapy for TIA; amlodipine for hypertension.
fix_detail.phrase_prefix_injected: I take clopidogrel 75mg once daily for my TIA and amlodipine 5mg for my blood pressure

cases.reference: Yes, I take clopidogrel and amlodipine.
cases.before: Yes, I take Copidogrel and Albert de Payne.
cases.after: Yes, I take clopidogrel and amlodipine.

fix_detail.conversation_context: Patient on dual antiplatelet + antihypertensive therapy post-TIA. Clinical context identifies clopidogrel + amlodipine as the expected drug pair.
fix_detail.phrase_prefix_injected: I take clopidogrel 75mg and amlodipine 5mg once daily
